summary: This sermon discusses the concept of reaching out to Allah and how He responds to His servants in real time. The speaker begins by addressing superstitions like the evil eye symbol and explains how such practices constitute shirk (associating partners with Allah). The main portion of the sermon focuses on Quranic verses and prophetic narrations that emphasize Allah's immediate responsiveness to His servants' calls and remembrance. The speaker cites hadiths from Sahih Muslim where Allah promises that when a servant reaches out to Him, He reciprocates exponentially—reaching out "a cubit" when the servant reaches "a hand span," and running when the servant walks. The sermon emphasizes that Allah responds not only on the Day of Judgment but in real time during a person's lifetime.
suggested_title: Allah's Immediate Response to His Servants: Building a Relationship with Allah
hadith_chapter: General narrations on Allah's responsiveness and the division of prayer
catchy_title: Does Allah Really Respond to You?
